title: High Resolution Imaging Mass Spectrometry Analysis using Bruker Daltonics Platforms
author: Elizabeth Neumann
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7gdhjs6

steps.title: Scann a 3200 dpi image of...
steps.instruction: Scann a 3200 dpi image of the matrix coated tissue section in the MTP slide adapter 2 with sufficient contrast to visualize the tissue boundaries.

steps.title: Choose a method that has "height...
steps.instruction: Choose a method that has "height correct" initiated upon sample docking.

steps.title: While the height correct profile is...
steps.instruction: While the height correct profile is being generated, open FlexImaging and follow the prompts to designate sample file name, method, pitch, scanned image of the slide, etc.

steps.title: Once the height correct profile has...
steps.instruction: Once the height correct profile has been generated, train the target position with three teaching points following the instructions within FlexImaging.

steps.title: Draw a measurement region around the tissue for IMS analysis
steps.instruction: Draw a measurement region around the tissue for IMS analysis.

steps.title: Go to an area off of...
steps.instruction: Go to an area off of the tissue and ensure the focus of the laser is adequate for 10 μm spatial resolution imaging.

steps.title: Insert the slide adapter plate into the instrument airlock
steps.instruction: Insert the slide adapter plate into the instrument airlock. Then press "load."
